versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: the last to crush his bones
18 Therefore this is what the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
“I will punish the king of Babylon and his land
as I punished the king of Assyria.
19 I will return Israel to his pasture,
and he will graze on Carmel and Bashan;
on the hills of Ephraim and Gilead.
20 In those days and at that time,
a search will be made for Israel’s guilt,
but they will not be found;

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 50**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Redemption for God’s People** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 50** remains the first control on meaning.
